top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Sovev Jerusalem's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
31 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Sovev Jerusalem's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 31 miles.
At Sovev Jerusalem, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
159ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Base fitness for Sovev Jerusalem. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Sovev Jerusalem. Train it until it feels boring.
Low-cadence force work for 4,921ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Sovev Jerusalem, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: May in Jerusalem, Israel can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Sovev Jerusalem. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Sovev Jerusalem. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and VO2max to prepare you for 31 miles of mixed terrain in Jerusalem, Israel.
